zh.lifeSummary: Thomasine Giles是John Heir Giles和Nell Gertrude Burrows的女兒，於1513年出生在英國肯特福利頓登。她於大約1539年與John Webb在英國英格蘭肯特福利頓登結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1593年在英國英格蘭肯特福利頓登享年80歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特福利頓登聖 馬也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Thomasine Giles是John Heir Giles和Nell Gertrude Burrows的女儿，于1513年出生在英国肯特福利頓登。她于大约1539年与John Webb在英国英格兰肯特福利頓登结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1593年在英国英格兰肯特福利頓登享年80岁，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特福利頓登聖 馬也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Giles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from a medieval personal name, Middle English Giles or Gile, a borrowing from Old French Gil(l)e(s). This is from Latin Aegidius and this presumably from Greek aigidion ‘kid, young goat’ (alternatively, it could be a Late Latin formation from the Latin personal name Eggius + the suffix -idius). The personal name was widely used in France and the Low Countries, partly through veneration of Saint Gilles de Provence, supposedly a hermit of the 7th century near Arles; he was patron saint of cripples, hence the dedication of Saint Giles Cripplegate in London, though the personal name itself was less common in England than elsewhere in Europe. See also Gilles .
Irish: adopted as an Anglicized equivalent of Gaelic Ó Glaisne, a County Louth name based on glas ‘green, blue, gray’.
French: variant of   Gilles  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Dellis
meanings: French (northern) and Walloon: habitational name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, denoting someone from Lys-lez-Lannoy in France (Nord) or Lies in the Belgian province of Brabant. Alternatively, of Flemish origin, from a variant of Dielis, a regional (Brabantine) vernacular form of the Latin personal name Aegidius (see Giles 1, compare Dils ).
Greek: variant of   Delis  .

variants.name: Gilles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Jacques, Pierre, Amie, Arsene, Berthony, Ghislaine, Gilles, Jean Marie, Jean-Baptiste, Maryse, Maxime, Odette.

French, Walloon, and Haitian: from the French personal name Gilles of which the original form was Latin Aegidius. This was the name of a 7th-century Provençal hermit, whose cult popularized the name in a variety of more or less mutilated forms: Gidi and Gidy in southern France, Gil(l)i in the area of the Alpes-Maritimes, and Gil(l)e elsewhere. This last form was taken over to England by the Normans (see Giles 1).
German: from the personal name Gilius, from Aegilius, a Late Latin variant of Aegidius (see Giles 1).

variants.name: Gilley
meanings: English: from a Middle English personal name recorded in Latin as Gilia, either a feminine form of Giles , or a pet form of Gillian .
English and Scottish: sometimes perhaps an English variant of Scottish   Gillie  ; see   Gillies  .

variants.name: Di Gilio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Angelo.
Italian: patronymic from the personal name Gilio, a variantof Egidio (see Giles ).

variants.name: D'Egidio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Giovanni, Nunzio, Camillo, Domenic, Pasquale.
Italian: patronymic from the personal name Egidio, from Latin Aegidius (see Giles ).

variants.name: Giel
meanings: German: from a medieval personal name of which the original form was Latin Aegidius (see Giles 1).

variants.name: Gilger
meanings: German: patronymic from a medieval personal name, a derivative of Aegilius, a later form of Latin Aegidius (see Giles 1).

variants.name: Gilio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Giuseppe.
Italian:
from the personal name Giglio, a derivative of the Latin personal name Aegidius (see Giles ).

variants.name: Gillard
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Gillard, Jillard, with initial soft g (/dʒ/), a borrowing of Old French Gillard, a pet form of Gille (see Giles and 4 below). Alternatively, from the Middle English personal name Gillard which, with a hard g (/g/), would be from Old French Guilard, corresponding to the Norman form Willard .
English: sometimes perhaps an altered form of   Gillett  .
English: sometimes perhaps an altered form of Gerard (Middle English Girard) with r/l substitution.

variants.name: Gil
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Juan, Francisco, Jesus, Jorge, Pedro, Mario, Miguel, Ana.  Portuguese Joao, Ligia.
Spanish, Catalan, and Portuguese: from the personal name Gil, from French Gille(s), from Latin Aegidius (see Giles 1).
German: from a vernacular short form of the medieval personal name Aegilius, a later form of Latin Aegidius (see Giles 1). Compare Gill 11.

nameMeanings.name: Thomasine
meanings: Hebrew: A twin; Thomasine is a female version.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: Thomasine Giles was born in 1513, in Frittenden, Kent, England as the daughter of John Heir Giles and Nell Gertrude Burrows. She married John Webb about 1539, in Frittenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1593, in Frittenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 80, and was buried in St Mary's Church, Frittenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé John Webb environ 1539, à Frittenden, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1593, à Frittenden, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 80 ans, et a été enterrée à St Mary's Church, Frittenden, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomasine Gilesは1513年にイギリスケントフリッテンデンでJohn Heir GilesとNell Gertrude Burrowsの娘として生まれた。約1539年にイギリスイギリスケントフリッテンデンでJohn Webbと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1593年にイギリスイギリスケントフリッテンデンで80歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスケントフリッテンデンスト マリ'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.Saint Mary's Church, Frittenden, Tunbrid: 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 프리텐든 스트 마이'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Thomasine Giles(은)는 1513년 영국 켄트 프리텐든에서 John Heir Giles(와)과 Nell Gertrude Burrows의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1539년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 프리텐든에서 John Webb(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1593년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 프리텐든에서 80세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 프리텐든 스트 마이'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Thomasine Giles nasceu em 1513, em Frittenden, Kent, Inglaterra filha de John Heir Giles e Nell Gertrude Burrows. Ela casou-se com John Webb aproximadamente 1539, em Frittenden, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1593, em Frittenden, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 80 anos, e foi sepultada em St Mary's Church, Frittenden, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Saint Mary's Church, Frittenden, Tunbrid: ст мэри'с чурч, фриттенден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Thomasine Giles родилась в 1513 г. в местности фриттенден, кэнт, Англия. Её родителей звали John Heir Giles и Nell Gertrude Burrows. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Webb около 1539 г. в местности фриттенден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1593 г. в местности фриттенден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 80 лет и была похоронена в местности ст мэри'с чурч, фриттенден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.
